detail.reasoning: The QC flag at 00:69:12 for 22 seconds of digital silence is covered by a production annotation (00:69:12–00:69:59) stating this is a deliberate audio blackout for dramatic effect, approved by the post supervisor. Per release policy RQ-CREATIVE-02, a creative annotation that covers the flagged range establishes the behavior as intentional and the flag is not a defect. The title is a tentpole releasing in 1 day with marketing locked, but because this is verified creative intent rather than an actual audio defect, no remediation is required. Record as no-defect and release as delivered.

The QC flag is a real minor caption sync defect (0.8s drift at 00:37:26) with no production annotation covering it. The title releases in APAC and EU only, so it is not CVAA-covered; therefore the accessibility hard block does not apply. However, the defect still requires remediation. Per policy RQ-FIX-04, timed-text defects can be repaired in-house in about 2 days, which matches the remaining 2-day window to premiere. In-house repair is the preferred path per RQ-PREF-00, and because this is a standard-tier title with a minor defect, escalation to the release board is unnecessary. Expediting an internal fix allows us to meet the marketed premiere without shipping a defective asset.

The QC flag is a major audio lip-sync defect (audio leads picture by ~4 frames at 00:29:56) with no production annotations covering the flagged timecode, so this is not an intentional creative choice. Per policy RQ-FIX-04, audio defects can be repaired in-house in about 2 days. The title has 3 days remaining until premiere (standard tier, marketing locked), so the in-house repair window fits comfortably within the release schedule. Per remediation preference order RQ-PREF-00, an in-house fix is preferred over vendor redelivery or delay. Therefore the flag is owned by audio-eng and the asset should be expedited through internal repair and re-validated before release.

The QC flag is a major severity colour-metadata mismatch in the package descriptor at 00:06:06, raised during spec-inspection. It is not a caption defect and no creative annotations cover the flagged range. Because the discrepancy is in the package descriptor rather than a picture defect baked into the video essence, it falls outside the scope of RQ-FIX-04’s vendor-redelivery requirement for essence-level picture defects. With 8 days remaining to premiere—well outside the 3-day release window—there is sufficient time for an in-house fix (estimated ~2 days for non-essence defects). The title is standard tier and marketing is not locked, so schedule risk is low. Per RQ-PREF-00 the preferred remediation is an in-house fix; therefore the flag is routed to video-eng for expedited internal correction of the package descriptor metadata before release.

The automated QC detected a major video-essence defect (compression artefacts at 00:85:26) with no production annotations covering the flagged timecode, so this is a genuine deliverable defect. Per policy RQ-FIX-04, picture defects baked into the video essence cannot be repaired in-house and require vendor redelivery (~5 days). The title is standard tier with 20 days remaining to premiere and marketing is not yet locked, giving sufficient schedule margin for a vendor redelivery without impacting the release window. Because the title is not tentpole, board escalation (RQ-BOARD-05) is not triggered. Assign to video-eng queue and request vendor redelivery.

The QC flag is a minor audio loudness drift (1.4 LU above spec) starting at 00:21:40, with no production annotations covering the flagged range. The title "Aster & Vale" (TTL-35102) is a tentpole releasing in UK/EU in 1 day with marketing already locked. Per release policy RQ-MINOR-03, minor defects inside the 3-day release window ship on time and are patched post-release. Because this is a minor non-caption defect and the premiere is only 1 day away, the correct course is to waive the flag for release and schedule an in-house audio repair for a post-release patch (audio-eng queue).

The flagged 22-second audio silence at 00:70:30 on AST-25702 is covered by a production annotation (range 00:70:30–00:70:59) stating this is intentional silence in the director’s cut, with no score or room tone. Per release policy RQ-CREATIVE-02, a creative-team annotation that covers the flagged timecode range establishes the behaviour as intentional and the flag is therefore not a defect. The title is not CVAA-covered, so no accessibility hard block applies. Record no-defect and release as delivered.

The QC flag is a major video-component defect raised at package-validation: the colour metadata in the package descriptor does not match the video essence at 00:41:07. The title "Sixteen Winters" (standard tier) is two days from its US premiere and marketing is already locked. Because the issue is a metadata mismatch in the package descriptor rather than a picture defect baked into the video essence, it falls within video-eng scope and may be repairable in house without re-encoding or vendor redelivery. Policy RQ-FIX-04 notes that picture defects baked into the essence require vendor redelivery (≈5 days), which would miss the 2-day window; however, a descriptor-level mismatch is not necessarily baked in, so an expedited internal fix is the appropriate first attempt. If video-eng determines the essence itself is at fault, the fallback would be vendor redelivery and a consequent delay, but for a standard-tier title the correct initial action is to task video-eng for an expedited repair before premiere.

The QC flag identifies major compression artefacts baked into the picture essence at 00:17:43 on tentpole title Glass Harbour (TTL-39469). There are no production annotations covering this timecode that would explain the issue as intentional creative intent. Per policy clause RQ-FIX-04, picture defects baked into the video essence cannot be repaired in-house and require vendor redelivery, which takes approximately 5 days. However, the title premieres in only 3 days (APAC and EU territories), making vendor redelivery impossible within the release window. Marketing is already locked. Per policy clause RQ-BOARD-05, a major in-house-unrepairable defect on a tentpole title with insufficient time for vendor redelivery is a commercial decision that must be escalated to the release board rather than unilaterally delaying a marketed premiere.

The QC flag identifies a genuine audio-essence defect (dialogue loudness drifting 1.4 LU above spec after 00:11:37) with no production annotation covering the flagged range, so it cannot be treated as intentional creative intent. Under RQ-PREF-00 the preferred remediation path is an in-house fix when the schedule permits. RQ-FIX-04 confirms audio defects can be repaired in-house in about 2 days. With 8 days remaining to the tentpole premiere in APAC/EU (non-CVAA) territories, there is sufficient time to complete the repair without delaying release or requiring vendor redelivery. Therefore the flag is routed to audio-eng for expedited internal correction before premiere.

The QC flag at 00:39:31 reports total audio loss, but a production annotation from the creative team covers the exact flagged range (00:39:31–00:39:59) and explicitly documents this as intentional silence: the director’s cut plays the scene without score or room tone. Per release-policy clause RQ-CREATIVE-02, a creative annotation that covers the flagged timecode range establishes the behavior as intentional and the flag is therefore not a defect. The title is 20 days from premiere with marketing not yet locked, so there is no schedule pressure forcing a different path. Record no-defect and release as delivered.

The QC flag is a confirmed caption defect (timed-text component, 800 ms drift across 14 events) on a title releasing in CVAA-covered territories (US, CA, UK). Per policy RQ-CVAA-01, caption defects in CVAA territories are a legal hard block that can never be waived, regardless of severity or premiere pressure. The title is a tentpole with marketing locked and only 1 day remaining to premiere, while in-house timed-text remediation requires approximately 2 days (RQ-FIX-04). Because the schedule does not allow an in-house fix before premiere, RQ-CVAA-01 mandates delaying the release rather than shipping with an uncorrected caption defect. The defect is not explained by any creative annotation, so it is a genuine timed-text issue requiring repair.

Major audio/video sync offset of 120ms sustained from 00:62:11 on standard-tier title "Paper Cities" (TTL-40154). No production annotation covers the flagged range, so this is not an intentional creative choice. Per policy RQ-FIX-04, audio defects can be repaired in-house in about 2 days, and the title has exactly 2 days until premiere in APAC/EU (non-CVAA) territories. Because the in-house repair fits the release window, we expedite an internal audio-eng fix rather than delay the marketed premiere or escalate to the board (escalation per RQ-BOARD-05 is reserved for tentpole titles where in-house repair is impossible).

The QC flag is a major video defect (colour metadata mismatch in the package descriptor at 00:34:53) on the tentpole title "Northwind" with only 3 days to premiere. No production annotation covers the flagged range, so creative intent does not explain the issue. Per policy RQ-FIX-04, picture defects baked into the video essence cannot be fixed in-house and require ~5 days for vendor redelivery. Even if this metadata mismatch might be addressable without re-encoding, the major severity and the locked marketing on a tentpole release create unacceptable commercial risk if we delay unilaterally. With insufficient time for vendor redelivery and no confirmed in-house remediation path that fits the window, this falls squarely under RQ-BOARD-05: a major defect on a tentpole title with too little time for vendor redelivery must be escalated to the release board for a commercial decision rather than delaying a marketed premiere.

Major picture-essence compression artefacts at 00:21:06 with no covering creative annotation. Board escalation per RQ-BOARD-05 is reserved for tentpole titles with insufficient lead time, which does not apply here.
